text: Recycling at home does not have to be difficult.
Start with the basics: separate paper, plastic, and glass into different bins.
Even this small gesture can make a significant difference over time.
Many people overlook items like batteries and old electronics, which should never go in regular rubbish.
Check if your local area has a collection point for these.
Another way to help the environment is to reduce food waste.
Plan your meals for the week and buy only what you need.
Leftover vegetables can be used to make soup rather than being thrown away.
Choosing sustainable products is also a practical step.
Look for items with less packaging or packaging that can be recycled readily.
These changes may seem subtle, but when an entire community adopts them, the impact is tremendous.
